findings.description: Back-imports from sibling behavioral modules: marketmind.pipeline.layer2_fundamental (line 11, static import) and marketmind.pipeline.layer1_narrative (line 68, deferred import inside _run_sector_drilldown). PICA §3.1 Rule 3 prohibits imports from sibling behavioral modules — imports must form a DAG: glue → modules → shared data types.
findings.remediation: ACCEPTED AS TEMPORARY — this is a compatibility adapter by design. If this module becomes permanent, refactor: extract the shared interface to a protocol/ABC in a shared-types module, or promote layer2_fundamental to a service layer that interactive adapters depend on.
findings.acceptance_rationale: Compatibility adapter wrapping layer2_fundamental for the older two-phase interactive API. Per docstring: 'New callers should use layer2_fundamental directly.'

findings.description: _select_strategy_group() function (lines 30-54) is defined but never called. _run_two_phase_l2() duplicates strategy selection logic inline (lines 177-193). Additionally, the 'yield from' usage is invalid in an async function.
findings.remediation: Remove _select_strategy_group() and _await_handler() if they remain unused.

findings.description: Multiple fallback paths converge on _confirm_single_phase(). Control flow is clear: try two-phase → on any failure/fallback → confirm single-phase. While loops have explicit exit conditions (return statements). No infinite loop risk.

summary: Import boundary violation (sibling behavioral module imports) — accepted as intentional for compatibility adapter role. Dead code (_select_strategy_group) should be cleaned up. Control flow and data flow are well-defined with clear entry point (run_l2_interactive) and clear inputs (SessionContext, cli_handler) / outputs (bool).
